name: DataBreach
description: A case template for handling suspected or confirmed data breach incidents, including validation, evidence preservation, scoping, containment, legal and privacy coordination, notification, remediation, and post-incident follow-up.
author: Zachary Carter
summary: Investigation of a suspected or confirmed data breach involving unauthorized access, disclosure, acquisition, misuse, or exfiltration of sensitive, regulated, or confidential information. Scope, affected data, access path, and legal or regulatory impact are under investigation.

tasks.title: Validate and classify breach event
tasks.description: Determine whether the incident involves confirmed unauthorized access, accidental disclosure, misdelivery, system compromise, insider misuse, third-party exposure, lost media, or a false positive. Distinguish suspected exposure from a confirmed breach event.

tasks.title: Initiate legal, privacy, compliance, and management coordination
tasks.description: Notify and coordinate with the appropriate internal stakeholders, including legal, privacy, compliance, management, security leadership, and affected business owners, to align investigative actions, retention, communications, and decision-making.

tasks.title: Identify affected systems, applications, accounts, and data
tasks.description: Determine the affected systems, applications, identities, data stores, repositories, and data sets. Document confirmed and suspected scope, including related business functions and third-party dependencies.

tasks.title: Preserve evidence and relevant records
tasks.description: Preserve relevant logs, system artifacts, access records, email records, endpoint evidence, cloud logs, database logs, DLP alerts, transfer records, and other artifacts needed to establish a defensible timeline and support investigation, legal review, and notification decisions.

tasks.title: Determine exposure, access, modification, and exfiltration scope
tasks.description: Assess whether data was merely exposed, actually accessed, copied, downloaded, shared, modified, deleted, or exfiltrated. Identify affected data types, sensitivity, likely time range, and any suspected access sources or recipients.

tasks.title: Identify access path and root cause
tasks.description: Investigate how the incident occurred, including compromised credentials, exploited systems, misconfiguration, insecure sharing, insider misuse, third-party access, lost devices, or other access paths. Document the suspected or confirmed root cause.

tasks.title: Contain affected systems, identities, and data access paths
tasks.description: Restrict unauthorized access, disable or limit affected accounts, revoke sessions, rotate credentials and keys, close exposed services or sharing paths, isolate affected systems where appropriate, and implement emergency controls to prevent further unauthorized activity.

tasks.title: Assess business, legal, privacy, and regulatory impact
tasks.description: Evaluate the impact to business operations, affected individuals, data confidentiality, data integrity, contractual obligations, regulatory exposure, and organizational risk based on the nature and sensitivity of the affected data.

tasks.title: Remediate vulnerabilities, access issues, and control gaps
tasks.description: Correct the underlying technical and procedural weaknesses that enabled the incident, including credential resets, permission changes, system patching, configuration fixes, data handling controls, and third-party access remediation.

tasks.title: Assess and execute notification obligations
tasks.description: Determine whether affected individuals, regulators, customers, partners, law enforcement, cyber insurance, or other parties must be notified based on legal, regulatory, contractual, privacy, or policy requirements, and document all decisions and communications.

tasks.title: Increase monitoring and validate containment
tasks.description: Increase monitoring on affected systems, identities, applications, and data access points. Validate that containment actions are effective and confirm that no continued unauthorized access, disclosure, or exfiltration is occurring.

tasks.title: Implement security improvements and close identified gaps
tasks.description: Improve controls related to access management, logging, retention, data handling, monitoring, segmentation, DLP, vulnerability management, and detection coverage based on the findings of the investigation.

tasks.title: Document findings and close case
tasks.description: Document the affected data, systems, access path, confirmed impact, containment actions, remediation steps, notification outcomes, and lessons learned. Confirm required actions are complete before case closure.

notes.title: Initial report
notes.content: Document how the possible data breach was identified, when it was discovered, and why the case was opened.

notes.title: Current status
notes.content: Track the current phase of the investigation, key updates, blockers, and next steps.

notes.title: Decision log
notes.content: Record major decisions, approvals, escalations, and significant events during the investigation.

notes.title: Stakeholders
notes.content: List involved stakeholders, including legal, privacy, compliance, business owners, management, and technical teams.

notes.title: Affected systems and applications
notes.content: Document the systems, applications, platforms, storage locations, and repositories involved in the incident.

notes.title: Affected accounts and roles
notes.content: Track user accounts, service accounts, privileged identities, third-party accounts, and access roles relevant to the case.

notes.title: Affected data sets
notes.content: List the data sets, files, records, databases, mailboxes, or repositories believed to be involved.

notes.title: Data sensitivity and classification
notes.content: Document the sensitivity and classification of the affected data, including regulated, confidential, customer, employee, financial, health, or operational data.

notes.title: Timeline of events
notes.content: Build a defensible timeline of relevant system, identity, application, and data-access activity.

notes.title: Access path and root cause
notes.content: Summarize how the incident occurred and document the suspected or confirmed root cause.

notes.title: Authentication and access findings
notes.content: Document login activity, permission changes, session behavior, remote access, sharing events, and other relevant access artifacts.

notes.title: Exfiltration and disclosure findings
notes.content: Summarize evidence of downloads, copying, exports, transfers, sharing, modification, deletion, or disclosure of affected data.

notes.title: Containment actions
notes.content: Document emergency controls, access restrictions, account actions, isolation steps, and other measures taken to stop ongoing impact.

notes.title: Credential and access changes
notes.content: Track password resets, key rotation, token revocation, permission changes, session invalidation, and third-party access restrictions.

notes.title: Remediation actions
notes.content: Summarize fixes applied to systems, applications, sharing settings, configurations, and security controls.

notes.title: Validation of containment
notes.content: Document how containment and remediation were validated and how continued unauthorized activity was ruled out or monitored.

notes.title: Legal and privacy coordination
notes.content: Track discussions, guidance, decisions, and hold requirements involving legal and privacy stakeholders.

notes.title: Notification decisions
notes.content: Record notification obligations, rationale, timelines, recipients, and approval decisions.

notes.title: Regulatory and contractual considerations
notes.content: Document applicable legal, contractual, regulatory, or policy requirements related to the incident.

notes.title: External communications
notes.content: Summarize communications with affected individuals, regulators, partners, customers, law enforcement, insurers, or other external parties.

notes.title: Findings summary
notes.content: Provide a concise summary of the incident, affected data, root cause, confirmed impact, and investigative conclusions.

notes.title: Business impact
notes.content: Document operational, legal, financial, privacy, customer, and reputational impact associated with the incident.

notes.title: Control improvements
notes.content: Document recommended improvements to access management, monitoring, logging, DLP, retention, segmentation, and incident response processes.

notes.title: Lessons learned
notes.content: Capture lessons learned, process gaps, detection gaps, and follow-up recommendations.
